name: Vimy to Victory Day Tour
tagline: Canada's greatest battles in the greatest war ever fought.
scheduleFull: Departs Arras every Tuesday, Friday and Sunday. Other days available on request.
heroAlt: The Canadian National Vimy Memorial illuminated at night
cardAlt: Preserved mine craters and trenches at Vimy Ridge
cardDesc: Vimy Ridge's preserved trenches and tunnels, the National Vimy Memorial, Hill 70, Monchy-le-Preux and the Hundred Days Offensive.
intro: Follow the trail from Vimy to victory during an unforgettable day on the battlefields of the Artois! Explore the preserved trenches, tunnels, and battlefield of Vimy Ridge before visiting the iconic Canadian National Vimy Memorial, one of Canada's most important national monuments.

After lunch, continue to the battlefields of Hill 70, Monchy-le-Preux, and the Hundred Days Offensive where Canadian and Newfoundland soldiers achieved some of the most remarkable victories of the war. Along the way, visit both iconic and lesser-known battlefield locations that bring you into the footsteps of soldiers and reveal the full and heroic story of Canada's contribution in the First World War.

itinerary.heading: Morning
items: Canadian Corps battle headquarters
Frontline trenches and underground tunnels on Vimy Ridge
Vimy Ridge battlefield and mine craters
Canadian National Vimy Memorial

itinerary.heading: Afternoon
items: Battle of Hill 70 Canadian battlefield & Memorial Park
Monchy-le-Preux battlefield and the Newfoundland Memorial
Drocourt-Quéant Line Canadian battlefield and Memorial at Dury
Canal du Nord battlefield (time permitting)

footnote: The above itinerary may be varied based on the wishes and consensus of the guests travelling on any particular day. This tour is also included within The Signature (2 Day) and The Ultimate (3 Day) Canadian Tours.

A guest visiting a Canadian war grave

name: In Flanders Fields Tour
scheduleFull: Departs Arras every Monday and Thursday. Other days available on request.
heroAlt: The Menin Gate Memorial illuminated at night in Ypres
cardAlt: The Menin Gate Memorial illuminated at night in Ypres
cardDesc: Passchendaele, Tyne Cot, the site where "In Flanders Fields" was written, and the nightly Last Post Ceremony.
intro: Journey through the infamous Flanders Fields battlefields and discover what inspired the war's most poignant poem. During the Great War, this small corner of Belgium witnessed some of the fiercest fighting of the First World War. Stand where Canadian soldiers stood facing chlorine gas clouds and went over the top through the mud and devastation of Passchendaele.

From the Christmas Truce battlefield of 1914 to the ridges and cemeteries of Flanders, this tour explores both the famous and not-so-famous side of Flanders Fields. Walk preserved trenches, visit battlefield memorials, and gain a deeper understanding of the sacrifices made by Canadian soldiers in Belgium. The day concludes with attendance at the moving nightly Last Post Ceremony beneath the Menin Gate Memorial in Ypres, where more than 54,000 missing Canadian and Commonwealth soldiers are commemorated.

itinerary.heading: Morning
items: Christmas Truce site at Ploegsteert Wood
Mount Sorrel, Canadian battlefield
Hill 62 Canadian Memorial

itinerary.heading: Afternoon
itinerary.note: Lunch is taken close to the Passchendaele battlefield.
items: Second Battle of Ypres gas attack battlefield
Brooding Soldier Memorial at St. Julien
Princess Patricia's Canadian Light Infantry battlefield at Frezenberg
Passchendaele battlefield and Crest Farm Memorial
Tyne Cot Commonwealth War Cemetery
Essex Farm — where John McCrae wrote "In Flanders Fields"

itinerary.heading: Evening
items: Menin Gate Memorial and the Last Post Ceremony

footnote: Tour itineraries may be adjusted to accommodate weather, timing, or the interests of guests. This tour is also included within The Signature (2 Day) and The Ultimate (3 Day) Canadian Tours.

name: The Somme Front Day Tour
tagline: Beaumont-Hamel, Courcelette, and the Battle of Amiens 1918.
scheduleFull: Departs Arras every Wednesday and Saturday. Other days available on request.
heroAlt: The Beaumont-Hamel Newfoundland Memorial caribou statue
cardAlt: The Beaumont-Hamel Newfoundland Memorial caribou statue
cardDesc: Beaumont-Hamel, Courcelette's original trenches, and the Battle of Amiens 1918.
intro: Explore the preserved battlefield of the Beaumont-Hamel Newfoundland Memorial, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and walk through original trenches and No Man's Land. Later, as we venture off the beaten path, we see what is left of Canada's bloodiest battlefield of Courcelette, where more than 24,000 Canadians were killed or wounded in 1916.

In the afternoon, visit the Amiens battlefield where the Canadian Corps spearheaded the offensive that would become the blackest day for the German army — marking the beginning of the end of the First World War.

itinerary.heading: Morning
items: Beaumont-Hamel Newfoundland Memorial
Original frontline trenches and No Man's Land
Courcelette battlefield and Canadian Victoria Cross locations
Original German command post bunker
Thiepval Memorial to the Missing

itinerary.heading: Afternoon
itinerary.note: Lunch is taken at Auchonvillers, close to the battlefield.
items: 1916 aid station at Auchonvillers
Red Baron crash site
Battle of Amiens 1918 Canadian battlefield
Hangard Wood and Victoria Cross location
Sir John Monash Centre (time permitting)

footnote: The above itinerary may be varied based on the wishes and consensus of the guests travelling on any particular day. This tour is also included within The Ultimate (3 Day) Canadian Tour.

name: The Signature — 2 Day Canadian Tour
tagline: In Flanders Fields + Vimy to Victory, combined.
scheduleFull: Departs Arras every Monday and Thursday. Other days available on request. Accommodation and meals are not included.
heroAlt: Misty morning at Ploegsteert Wood, the Christmas Truce site
cardAlt: Misty morning at Ploegsteert Wood, the Christmas Truce site
cardDesc: Includes In Flanders Fields + Vimy to Victory. The top-to-bottom of Canada's action on the Western Front.
intro: The Signature — 2 Day Canadian Tour combines our two most popular day tours to cover the top-to-bottom of Canada's action on the Western Front.

All tours are based in the old town of Arras. We can recommend accommodation in hotels or guest houses our clients have happily stayed at. Pick-up/drop-off is at your accommodation in Arras or at the train station.

itinerary.heading: Day 1 — In Flanders Fields
itinerary.note: North into Belgium, visiting some of the most iconic battlegrounds of the First World War — original trenches, the infamous Passchendaele battlefield, and the site where John McCrae wrote "In Flanders Fields" during the gas attacks of 1915. A relaxing dinner in the old town of Ypres leads into the emotive Last Post Ceremony at the Menin Gate, where more than 6,000 missing Canadians are commemorated.
items: Christmas Truce site at Ploegsteert Wood, Mount Sorrel & Hill 62
Passchendaele battlefield, Crest Farm Memorial & Tyne Cot Cemetery
Essex Farm and the Menin Gate Last Post Ceremony

itinerary.heading: Day 2 — Vimy to Victory
itinerary.note: Into the footsteps of Canadian troops at Vimy Ridge, Hill 70, and the pivotal battles of 1918 on the famous Cambrai road — including the unforgettable National Vimy Memorial, where more than a century on, Canada's efforts and sacrifices are still proudly remembered by local communities.
items: Frontline trenches, tunnels & mine craters on Vimy Ridge
Canadian National Vimy Memorial
Hill 70, Monchy-le-Preux & the Drocourt-Quéant Line

footnote: The above itinerary may be varied based on the wishes and consensus of the guests travelling on any particular day. Customised tours are available for this area on request.

name: The Ultimate — 3 Day Canadian Tour
tagline: Vimy to Victory + In Flanders Fields + The Somme Front, combined.
scheduleFull: Departs Arras every Monday and Thursday. Other days available on request. Accommodation and meals are not included.
heroAlt: The Thiepval Memorial to the Missing of the Somme
cardAlt: The Thiepval Memorial to the Missing of the Somme
cardDesc: Includes Vimy to Victory + In Flanders Fields + The Somme Front. From the 1915 gas attacks to the victories of 1918.
intro: The Ultimate — 3 Day Canadian Tour combines our three day tours to take you into the complete footsteps of Canadian troops on the frontlines of the First World War. From the 1915 gas attacks, to the brutal Somme, up to Vimy Ridge and onto the victories of 1918, where Canadians fought as shock troops to bring the war to an end.

All tours run from the old town of Arras. We can recommend accommodation in 3+ star hotels or guest houses our clients have happily stayed at. Pick-up/drop-off is at your accommodation in Arras or at Arras Train Station.
